news.text: Trump threatens Iran with power plant strikes over Hormuz oil blockade - Reuters
Saudi Arabia orders Iranian military attache, four embassy staff to leave - Reuters
Family of Iranian butcher missing in strike on commercial complex clings to hope - Reuters
3 themes that gripped Wall Street as stocks dropped for a 4th straight week
Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports if DHS shutdown doesn't end, while Elon Musk offers to cover TSA agents' pay
Britain says Cyprus base will not be part of UK-US self-defence deal - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Risk-off sentiment dominating pre-market with SPY down 1.7% to $648.57 as geopolitical tensions escalate between Trump administration and Iran over Hormuz Strait oil blockade. VIX spiking 6.51% to 35.97 signals genuine fear creeping back into markets after four consecutive weeks of selling pressure.

◆ What to Watch

  • Iran-US Tensions: Trump's threat of power plant strikes following oil blockade could trigger energy sector volatility and broader risk-off moves
  • DHS Shutdown Impact: Airport disruptions from government shutdown creating additional market uncertainty with Trump threatening ICE deployment
  • Saudi Diplomatic Response: Expulsion of Iranian embassy staff escalating Middle East tensions beyond US-Iran bilateral issues
  • Weekend Gap Fills: Monitor if major indices attempt to fill opening gaps or continue selling pressure from Friday's close
⚠ Bear Case
Geopolitical risk premium expanding with Iran blockade threatening 20% of global oil supply. VIX above 35 typically signals more downside ahead as institutional deleveraging accelerates.

◆ Sector Rotation

Defensive rotation underway with Financials (+0.18%) the only green sector as rate expectations shift on geopolitical uncertainty. Utilities (-4.06%) and Real Estate (-3.17%) getting hammered despite defensive characteristics, suggesting broad-based selling rather than flight to safety.
⚡ Key Driver
Oil supply disruption fears driving the entire risk-off trade. Energy sector surprisingly flat (-0.08%) suggests market pricing in resolution or strategic reserve releases.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $645: Critical support from February lows - break triggers acceleration toward $620
  • QQQ $575: Next major support after breaking Friday's $582 level
  • VIX $40: Resistance level that typically marks capitulation phases
  • XLE $85: Energy breakout level if oil supply concerns intensify

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
